Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Intermediate Operation Triangulation is targeting the Healthcare sector with intermediate-level attacks, aiming to disrupt operations and steal sensitive patient data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Account takeover, Phishing, Credential compromise

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-20198 [CWE-22]: iMessage zero-click vulnerability exploited via spear-phishing attachment.
stage.description: The attackers leverage social engineering through a phishing email containing a malicious iMessage attachment. This attachment exploits a zero-click vulnerability (CVE-2023-20198) to gain initial access to the target's device.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-38107 [CWE-94]: Arbitrary command execution via built-in scripting capabilities on compromised iOS device.
stage.description: Once access is gained, the attackers use built-in scripting capabilities on the compromised iOS device to execute arbitrary commands, enabling them to move laterally within the network.

Scenario.summary: Operation Triangulation initiates their attack against the Healthcare sector using a spear-phishing campaign targeting employees. Once initial access is gained, they exploit vulnerabilities to execute commands and persistently maintain access to the compromised devices. The ultimate goal is to escalate privileges, evade defenses, and impact the target by destroying or encrypting sensitive patient data.
